title: Manchester City vs Liverpool FA Cup Quarterfinal LIVE Updates: Score, Analysis, and Highlights on NBC Sports
body:
Manchester City Dominates Liverpool in FA Cup Quarterfinal Rout

Manchester City secured a resounding 4-0 victory over Liverpool in their FA Cup quarterfinal matchup at the Etihad Stadium on Saturday, sending the Reds crashing out of the competition. The convincing win sets up a tantalizing prospect for the Citizens, who are now one step closer to a coveted FA Cup semifinal spot. Erling Haaland's clinical hat-trick was the standout performer, but it was a collective effort from the Manchester City team that left Liverpool reeling.

The first half of the match saw both teams create opportunities, but it was Manchester City who finally broke the deadlock in the 53rd minute. Erling Haaland, the Norwegian striker, scored his first goal of the afternoon with a precision-finished header from a corner kick. The Citizens continued to push for more, and their persistence paid off when Antoine Semenyo netted a beautiful strike in the 67th minute. Semenyo's goal was a testament to the team's attacking prowess, as they continued to dominate the proceedings.

Haaland, however, was the one who stole the show, scoring two more goals in quick succession in the 77th and 80th minutes. His second goal was a stunning individual effort, as he showcased his exceptional pace and agility to leave the Liverpool defenders in his wake. His third and final goal was a masterclass in finishing, as he slotted the ball past the Liverpool goalkeeper with ease. With Haaland's hat-trick, Manchester City effectively sealed their fate, leaving Liverpool to rue their poor defending.

The result marks a significant blow to Liverpool's FA Cup hopes, which had been building momentum in recent weeks. With the Reds now eliminated from the competition, their focus will shift to the Premier League and Champions League, where they will look to regroup and bounce back from this defeat. Manchester City, on the other hand, will be buoyed by this victory, which comes at a crucial juncture in their season. They have now won 10 FA Cup titles, and with this win, they will look to add to their trophy cabinet.

The Citizens' attacking prowess has been a key factor in their success this season, with Haaland's goal-scoring form being a major contributor. His partnership with his teammates has been a joy to watch, and their chemistry on the pitch is starting to bear fruit. As Manchester City look to the semifinals, they will be confident in their ability to overcome any opposition, given their current form.

Looking ahead, Manchester City will now face a tough test in the semifinals, where they will be joined by other top-tier clubs vying for the coveted FA Cup title. The competition will undoubtedly be fierce, but with their current form and confidence, the Citizens will be looking to make a deep run in the tournament. The stage is set for a thrilling conclusion to the FA Cup, and Manchester City will be one of the teams to watch as they bid to lift the trophy.
